Compute external linear predictions and aggregate multiple external models

calcExtY.Rd

Compute external-model linear predictions on a target cohort and, when more than one external model is supplied, aggregate them into a single combined prediction. Aggregation is controlled by multi.method: "ind" keeps each model independent, "PCA" aggregates via the first principal component of the normalised external coefficients, and "stacking" learns optimal stacking weights from the target data via family-specific likelihood maximisation.

Usage

calcExtY(X, y, beta.external, family, multi.method, optim.args = list())

Arguments

X

An n x p numeric matrix of target-cohort predictors.

y

A numeric response vector of length n.

beta.external

A (p+1) x M matrix of external model coefficients. The first row is the intercept; remaining rows are predictor coefficients.

family

A string: "gaussian", "binomial", or "poisson".

multi.method

A string: "ind", "PCA", or "stacking".

optim.args

A list of arguments passed to optim() when using stacking with binomial or Poisson families. See stacking_binomial and stacking_poisson for available options.

Value

A list with two elements:

beta.external

The external coefficient matrix. For "PCA", this is a (p+1) x 1 aggregated coefficient vector; for "ind" and "stacking", this is the original input.

y.external

The n x M' matrix of external linear predictions on the response scale, where M' = 1 for "PCA" and "stacking" and M' = M for "ind".

Details

Used internally by BRIERi, BRIERi.cv, and BRIERfull, but exposed for users who want to compute external predictions independently of the BRIER fitting framework.

See also

BRIERi, BRIERi.cv, BRIERfull, stacking_gaussian, stacking_binomial, stacking_poisson

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
set.seed(1)
n <- 200
p <- 50
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), ncol = p)
beta_true <- c(rep(1, 5), rep(0, p - 5))
y <- X %*% beta_true + rnorm(n)

# one external model (intercept + p coefficients)
beta.external <- matrix(c(0, beta_true * 0.8), ncol = 1)
ext <- calcExtY(X, y, beta.external, family = "gaussian", multi.method = "ind")
head(ext$y.external)
} # }
